name: Adhikaar
description: Adhikaar, meaning “rights” in Nepali, is a women-led community and workers’ center that provides direct services to the Nepali-speaking* community and organizes low-income workers and impacted community members to promote social justice and human rights.

name: AKKA (Association of Kannada Kootas of America)
Alabama Nepalese Association (ALNA)
description: Bring Nepalese Together in The Great State of Alabama and Beyound

name: Alliance of South Asians Taking Action (ASATA)
description: ASATA is a San Francisco Bay Area all-volunteer grassroots group working to educate, organize, and empower the Bay Area South Asian communities to end violence, oppression, racism and exploitation within and against our diverse communities.

name: America Nepal Helping Society
description: America Nepal Helping Society (ANHS) is an American Nepali community based association dedicated in promotion and the preservation of Nepali customs, traditions, culture, religious festivities and language predominantly in DMV (Washington DC, Virginia & Maryland) metropolitan area.

name: America Nepal Medical Association (ANMA)
description: ANMA is committed to empowering Nepali physicians across the US through fostering a robust network, promoting education and professional development, advocating for policies that uplift Nepali communities, and facilitating collaboration between Nepali and American medical societies.

name: America Nepal Society (ANS)
description: America Nepal Society is a nonprofit organization working for fostering greater knowledge and understanding of literature, arts, cultures, values, manners, social customs, and other facts of diversities and similarities among Americans and Nepalese in the USA.

name: American Association of Bangladeshi Engineers and Architects, Inc., (AABEA)
description: American Association of Bangladeshi Engineers and Architects, Inc., (AABEA) (www.aabea.org) is a non-profit professional organization to provide services to its members as well as greater Bangladeshi community in various areas in North America.

name: American Association of Physicians of Indian Origin
American Pakistan Foundation (APF)
description: The American Pakistan Foundation (APF) empowers the Pakistani American community and its friends through fostering leadership, youth development and civic engagement, while building bridges between the people of the United States and Pakistan.

name: American Pakistani Public Affairs Committee
description: APPAC was founded in 2017 with the mission of creating resources not only for Pakistani Americans but for all Marginalized communities nationally. Our objective is to provide a substantial amount of support for Pakistani and Muslim Americans to run for their local town, city, and states race and win.

name: American Society of Nepalese Engineers (ASNEngr)
description: American Society of Nepalese Engineers (ASNEngr) provides a platform for Nepalese, and their friends, in engineering and closely related scientific and technical areas to come together, exchange ideas, and support each other for their and the larger society’s common good.

name: American South Asian Network (ASAN)
description: The American South Asian Network (ASAN), is dedicated to elevating, empowering, and uniting South Asians across the United States. We support South Asian creators, entrepreneurs, and leaders to foster community solidarity, cultural representation, and socioeconomic equity. Through innovative programs and strategic partnerships, ASAN celebrates excellence, drives impactful change, and reshapes narratives within and beyond the South Asian diaspora.

name: Apna Ghar
description: Apna Ghar provides critical, comprehensive, culturally competent services, and conducts outreach and advocacy across communities to end gender violence.

name: Arkansas Nepali Society (ANS)
description: ANS represents the Nepali community residing in Little Rock, the capital city of Arkansas. Our overarching objective is to foster a cohesive Nepali community in Arkansas, preserving our culture, values, and heritage while also actively engaging in serving the broader community around us.

name: Ashiyanaa
description: Ashiyanaa (formerly ASHA for Women) provides culturally-competent support services to South Asian women and children impacted by interpersonal violence (also known as domestic violence). Over the years, our services and programs have expanded and we currently serve all South Asians with origins in Afghanistan, Bhutan, Bangladesh, India, Nepal, Pakistan and Sri Lanka.

name: Association of Nepalese Agricultural Professionals of Americas (NAPA)
description: The Association of Nepalese Agricultural Professionals of Americas (NAPA) is a non-profit, non-governmental, non-religious, and non-political professional organization dedicated to serve mankind through educational, scientific, and developmental initiatives.

name: Association of Nepalese in Midwest America (ANMA)
description: The mission of the Association of Nepalese in Midwest America (ANMA) is to promote, coordinate, and facilitate close cooperation among Nepalese and friends of Nepal in North America, particularly in the Midwest Region of the United States, for maintaining and fostering Nepalese identity and cultural heritage.

name: Association of Nepalese Mathematicians in America (ANMA)
description: The Association of Nepalese Mathematicians in America (ANMA) is a nonprofit education and research oriented organization, formed by Nepalese mathematicians and statisticians in the United States of America, with goals to gather and disseminate accomplishments and experiences in teaching and research of Nepalese mathematicians and statisticians in Nepal, the United States and around the world.

name: Association of Nepali Physicists in America (ANPA)
description: The Association of Nepali Physicists in America (ANPA) is a nonprofit membership organization dedicated to furthering physical science education and sharing physics research among Nepali physicists around the globe through various scientific activities.

name: Association of Nepali Teraian in America (ANTA)
description: ANTA is an autonomous, independent, non-political, non-profit organization to provide a sense of community and identity for Teraians through humanitarian projects in Nepal and community-building activities in the US.

name: Association of Nepalis in Minnesota
description: ANMN's mission is to build, preserve and promote an enthusiastic, active and supportive community among the Nepalis and friends of Nepal in the state of Minnesota.

name: Association of Nepalis in the Americas (ANA)
description: Promote unity and harmony among the Nepali Diaspora living in the Americas, foster Nepali identity & culture, and empower with knowledge to succeed.

name: Association of Pakistani Americans of Bolingbrook
description: Our mission is to unite our community and to make positive contributions through education and service.

name: Association of Pakistani Physicians of New England
Association of Physicians of Pakistani Descent of North America
Association of Physicians of Pakistani Descent of North America Arizona Chapter
Association of Physicians of Pakistani Descent of North America Georgia Chapter
Association of Physicians of Pakistani Descent of North America in Saint Louis, Missouri
Association of Physicians of Pakistani Descent of North America Maryland Chapter
Association of Physicians of Pakistani Descent of North America North Texas Chapter
description: To help Pakistani physicians practice medicine in the north Texas area.

name: Association of Physicians of Pakistani Descent of North America Pediatrics
description: The group aims to bring Pediatricians and Pediatric specialists in North America together to know each other better, and help other pediatric colleagues, new-comer Pediatricians from Pakistan, as well neonates, children and adolescents in North America and Pakistan.

name: Association of Physicians of Pakistani Descent of North America South Texas Chapter
Association of Physicians of Pakistani Descent of North America Southern California Chapter
Association of Physicians of Pakistani-Descent of North America Greater Cincinnati
Baltimore Association of Nepalese in America (BANA)
description: The Baltimore Association of Nepalese in America (BANA) is dedicated to preserving Nepali culture in the U.S. Based in Baltimore, BANA promotes Nepali traditions and fosters fellowship among Nepali Americans and other communities in the area. As a leading organization, it works for the welfare of the Nepali American community in Baltimore and Maryland, partnering with local and state governments on various empowerment programs and projects.

name: Bangali-American Christian Association (BACA)
description: Bangali-American Christian Association (Baca) was born with the Bangali-American Christian inhabitants of the Washington Metropolitan area to form one united organization to foster unity, solidarity, peace, and progress through educational, cultural, religious, social, and charitable activities to establish a strong united platform for building a better community.

name: Bangladesh Association for Greater Kansas City
description: The mission of the Bangladesh Association for Greater Kansas City (BAGKC) is to promote and preserve the Bangladeshi culture and heritage in the Greater Kansas City area.

name: Bangladesh Association of America, Inc (BAAI)
description: The Bangladesh Association of America, Inc. (BAAI) is a voluntary association dedicated to promoting a wide range of social, literary, cultural, educational, religious, and humanitarian activities. Through a diverse array of cultural events, festivals, and performances, BAAI creates opportunities for community members to celebrate their roots and share the rich traditions of Bangladesh with others.

name: Bangladesh Association of Georgia (BAG)
description: Founded with a vision to unite and empower Bangladeshi-Americans in Georgia, BAG has been a beacon of cultural preservation and social integration since its inception.

name: Bangladesh Association of Greater Austin (BAGA)
description: Bangladesh Association of Greater Austin (BAGA) is a non-profit organization  to promote and sustain cultural, charitable, educational, and community services on behalf of the Bangladeshi-American community living in greater Austin.

name: Bangladesh Association of Michigan - BAM
description: Bangladesh Association of Michigan - BAM is a community organization established to foster a sense of unity, support, and cultural exchange among individuals of Bangladeshi origin residing in Michigan, USA. It was founded on the principles of inclusivity and cultural preservation.

name: Bangladesh Association of New England (BANE)
description: Bangladesh Association of New England (BANE) strives to be a cohesive organization for all New Englanders of Bangladeshi origin with intent to celebrate Bangladesh’s rich history, language, culture and heritage.  BANE is also intent on providing an environment facilitating the relatively smooth integration of newly arrived Bangladeshis into the New England region.  BANE shall provide a platform for creating the environment for cultinvating the love for Bangladesh into our next generation of Bangladeshi Americans.

name: Bangladesh Association Of North Texas (BANT DFW)
description: Promoting Bangladeshi culture, fostering community connections, & celebrating diversity in North TX.

name: Bangladesh Association of Phoenix
description: Bangladesh Association of Phoenix (BAP) is a non-profit socio-cultural organization of Bangladeshis living in Arizona.

name: Bangladesh Law Society USA
description: Bangladesh Law Society USA Inc (BLS) is an organization of Bangladeshi who obtained the law degree and living in USA. Our vision is to become a successful non-profit organization highly accessible to all members of our community.

name: Bangladesh Medical Association of North America
description: Bangladesh Medical Association of North America (BMANA) is a membership organization to bring together and to improve communication between the physicians who are of Bangladeshi origin or have trained in the various Medical and Dental Colleges of Bangladesh, and are currently residents of North America, and other physicians.

name: Bangladesh Society Inc.
description: The prime mission of Bangladesh Society Inc. is to lead our community to a parallel way alongside the mainstream of USA through mutual cooperation and amity. The other mission of the society is to assimilate our new generation with our own culture, tradition, social and religious values so that they themselves preserve and practice these in their daily life.

name: Bangladesh-American Society of Greater Houston
description: The Bangladesh-American Society of Greater Houston provides for the intellectual, cultural, physical, social, spiritual, and civic welfare to people of Bangladeshi origin; promotes understanding, goodwill, and appreciation of cultures of Bangladesh among all persons regardless of color, creed, race, religion, and national origin; promotes Bengali-American relations; promotes cultural exchange with other groups, organizations, or institutions interested in Bengali culture; and facilitates activities of other professional, student, or any other group of Bangladeshis,

name: Bangladeshi American Advocacy Group (BAAG)
description: The Bangladeshi American Advocacy Group (BAAG) was founded to create a platform for Bangladeshis in the state of New York to demand equal political rights and to raise their quality of life.

name: Bangladeshi American Association of Connecticut (BAAC)
description: Bangladeshi American Association of Connecticut (BAAC) was founded to provide social support to expatriate Bangladeshis in need- including enhancing their communication skills, assisting them with various vocational training that can improve their skills in the United States, introducing various scholarship programs and continue to provide much needed health care services to the expatriates where they may find it challenging to get such services through U.S. government agencies and facilities.

name: Bangladeshi American Bar Association (BABA)
description: The Bangladeshi American Bar Association (BABA) is a voluntary bar association dedicated to the needs, concerns, and interests of the Bangladeshi American legal community all over the USA. In pursuit of its objectives, BABA regularly organizes panels, receptions, networking events, community outreach efforts, public service projects, mentoring programs, political awareness campaigns, and a variety of other activities.

name: Bangladeshi American Business Society of Hillside Avenue (BABSHA)
description: Bangladeshi American Business Society of Hillside Avenue (BABSHA) is a merchant association based in Jamaica Queens

name: Bangladeshi American Community Development and Youth Services (BACDYS)
Bangladeshi American Foundation Inc. (BAF)
description: The Bangladeshi American Foundation Inc. (BAF) mission is to get every person to interact and feel passionate at the cultural events and the community works by creating interactions, networking and bringing value to the Bangaldeshi American community.

name: Bangladeshi American Law Enforcement Foundation
description: Our vision is to prevent delinquency & crime among Bangladeshi American community. Encourage civic engagement &  integration while maintaining our heritage.

name: Bangladeshi American Police Association (BAPA)
description: The founding principle of Bangladeshi American Police Association (BAPA) is to share information about our collective experiences for the betterment of fellow Law Enforcement Officers in addition to building a bridge between the Bangladeshi community and Law Enforcement.

name: Bangladeshi American Postal Employee’s Association USA
Bangladeshi American Public Affairs Committee (BAPAC)
description: Bangladeshi American Public Affairs Committee (BAPAC) The BAPAC was formed to promote the values and principles of the United States Constitution among the Bangladeshi Americans; to enhance the awareness of the rights and responsibilities as citizens of the United States; and to provide Bangladeshi Americans with an institution to legally organize as a group to exercise their political rights.

name: Bangladeshi American Social & Cultural Organization WNY INC. - BASCO WNY
description: As an immigrant community in USA, Bangladesh and it’s rich social and cultural heritages are always in our heart. Bangladeshi American Social & Cultural Organization (BASCO WNY) was formed to bring together Bangladeshis living in Western New York by prompting our culture and values of Independence.

name: Bangladeshi American Society (BAS)
description:
Bangladeshi American Society is a non-profit, non-political whose goals are to 
provide cultural, intellectual, social, physical, spiritual, and charitable welfare to the people of Bangladeshi origin in the USA and promote goodwill, understanding and appreciation of cultural heritage of Bangladesh among all persons in the United States of America.

name: Bangladeshi American Women’s Development Initiative (BAWDI)
description: Bangladeshi American Women’s Development Initiative (BAWDi)’s mission is to promote, support, and address the unmet needs of Bangladeshi women and children in our Paterson community and throughout New Jersey through grassroots organizing, connection to services, advocacy, disseminating information and educating community members on social issues, and cultivating a safe space for sisterhood.

name: Bangladeshi Americans for Political Progress (BAPP)
description: Bangladeshi Americans for Political Progress (BAPP) is a grassroots movement to create a Bangladeshi-American voting bloc in New York to elect political candidates and advocate for legislation that protect and advance the well-being of working class and low-income communities. We do this by: educating our community about the political process; increasing electoral participation; and forming alliances with other progressive organizations.

name: Bangladeshi Expatriate Society of Texas (BEST)
description: Bangladesh Expatriate Society of Texas (BEST) was formed to be the premier organization for Bangladeshi Americans living in Texas by: - Helping the community with social services - Supporting recent immigrations with their assimilation into mainstream American society - Fostering an environmental of unity and, - Promoting and preserving Bangladeshi heritage and culture.

name: Bangladeshi Humanitarian Aid and Leadership Outreach (BHALO)
description: BHALO derives from the Bengali word meaning "good," which is what we strive to do. Beginning with our own humble neighborhood in Queens, New York, the Bangladeshi Humanitarian Aid and Leadership Outreach aims to empower, unite and elevate the people within our communities.

name: Bay Area Bangladesh Association (BABA)
description: Bay Area Bangladesh Association (BABA) seeks to empower children through art, sports, and cultural experiences and keep the cultural heritage of Bangladesh alive among the Bangladeshi residents in Northern California

name: Bengali  Association  of  San Diego,  California
Bengali American Boldest Association (BABA)
description: Bengali American Boldest Association (BABA) was formed with the vision to support both uniformed and non-uniformed, active or retired members of the NYC Department of Correction. Our mission is to empower, educate, and reinforce their understanding of the department’s rules and regulations, and to keep them abreast with the newly accepted practices and procedures.

name: Bengali American Hindu Society
description: Bengali American Hindu Society conducts, coordinates and promotes the community, cultural and social activities among the people of Hindu origin in the USA and the people of other cultures on behalf of the Bengali community. It fosters friendship and understanding between the people of Bengali origin, Indian origin, and the Americans.

name: Bengali Community of Greater Washington Metropolitan Area
Bengali Cultural Society (BCS) of Cleveland, OH
Bengalis of New York (BONY)
description: Bengalis of New York (BONY) is a platform to highlight stories about the growing Bengali diaspora and to share stories and experience

name: Burmese Nepali Association of USA
description: The Burmese Nepali Association of USA is dedicated to empowering and supporting (Burma) Myanmar-born Nepali people in the USA and across the globe. We promote cultural heritage, education, social integration, and economic well-being by fostering unity, building a strong network, and advocating for the rights and interests of our community.

name: Burmese Rohingya Community of Georgia
description: The Burmese Rohingya Community of Georgia Inc, is a community-based social service organization aimed at serving the needs of the Rohingya refugee population in greater Atlanta, Georgia. The organization also focuses on raising awareness of the persecution of the Rohingya in Burma (Myanmar).

name: Central Desi
description: We are a local news organization in New Jersey that exclusively covers the South Asian community. We are inclusive of all Desi identities.

name: Central Ohio Nepali Association (CONA)
description: Central Ohio Nepali Association (CONA) is a local non-profit 501(c)3 organization of people of Nepali Origin currently living in the Greater Columbus area in Ohio. It includes Nepalis from Nepal, Bhutani-Nepali, Burmeli-Nepali, or other Nepalis who have migrated to Central Ohio from any parts of the world.

name: Chhaya CDC
description: Chhaya was founded to address the housing and economic needs for low-income South Asian and Indo-Caribbean New Yorkers.

name: Chicagoland Nepali Friendship Society (CNFS)
description: CNFS exists to improve overall quality of life of Nepalese immigrants in Chicagoland, ensuring their dignity, respect and well-being, through education, training and developing capabilities by empowering individuals to achieve their fullest potential through a broad spectrum of services; create better lives for themselves and their families while strengthening community action and civic engagement, foster their participation on keeping intact their culture and social values in America.

name: Connecticut Association of Physicians of Pakistani descent of North America
Council of Pakistan American Affairs (COPAA)
description: COPAA works to promote the civic & civil rights and well-being of the Pakistani American community. COPAA endeavors to achieve these goals through community engagement, youth leadership training and coalition-building with diverse communities.

name: Daya
description: Daya's mission is to empower South Asian survivors of domestic and sexual violence through culturally specific services and to educate the community in an effort to end the cycle of abuse.

name: Desi LGBTQ Helpline
description: DeQH is an all-volunteer collective dedicated to strengthening wellness and reducing isolation by connecting LGBQ/TGNB+ South Asians to communities and resources that support and promote healing and thriving. We provide free, confidential, culturally sensitive peer support by and for LGBQ/TGNB+ South Asians in the United States.​

name: Desi Rainbow Parents & Allies
description: Desi Rainbow Parents & Allies was conceived for South Asian families and friends of lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer and questioning individuals to learn about LGBTQIA+ issues, find community with one another and learn how to better support their loved ones. We serve Desi individuals and families who trace their origins to South Asia, including India, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Nepal, Bhutan, and the Maldives.  Our mission is to foster understanding and acceptance among families, with the goal of affirming and celebrating our LGBTQIA+ loved ones.

name: Desis Rising Up and Moving (DRUM)
description: DRUM was founded in 2000 to build the power of South Asian and Indo-Caribbean low wage immigrant workers, youth, and families in New York City to win economic and educational justice, and civil and immigrant rights.

name: Dhroopad
description: DHROOPAD is a cultural organization in the greater Washington DC Metro area with an objective to promote the rich Bengali cultural heritage, and to foster arts and literature as a force that transcends social, cultural and religious barriers and instigate compassion for humanity.

name: Division on South Asian Americans (DoSAA) of Asian American Psychological Association
description: DoSAA is a community of students and professionals committed to understanding social, emotional, political, and personal influences affecting South Asians in psychology and creating a forum to impact change for the betterment of South Asian mental health. DoSAA intends to be a credible source of information and resources on South Asian mental health issues.

name: Equality Labs
description: Equality Labs consists of a team of dedicated civil rights activists, caste equity organizers, technologists, and movement builders. We center the leadership of South Asian caste-oppressed, queer, and religious minority communities in an ongoing redefinition of South Asian identity across the world.

name: Federation of Bangladeshi Associations in North America (FOBANA)
description: FOBANA®, is an umbrella organization for Bangladeshi Associations in North America to preserve and promote the language, culture and heritage of Bangladesh.

name:
Federation of Tamil Sangams of North
  America (FETNA)
description: FETNA is an umbrella organization of Tamil Sangams whose primary objective is the preservation and growth of Tamil language, culture, and community.

name: Georgia Association of Physicians of Indian heritage
Global Nepali Professional Network (GNPN)
description: GNPN is an American organization dedicated to the professional development of its members and technological progress of Nepal.

name: Gorkha Samaj USA
description: Bring together the individuals with Gorkhali background or have interest in Gorkha for growth, development, education, health, safety, and welfare of the people living in Gorkha Nepal and USA and supporting them through knowledge, resources, and technology.

name: Grace And Peace Mission To Orissa
description: Grace And Peace Mission To Orissa is a faith-based organization serving the Odia community in New York, likely providing spiritual guidance and social support to members of Odia descent.

name: Greater Boston Nepali Community (GBNC)
description: The Greater Boston Nepali Community (GBNC) is a thriving association of Nepalis and friends of Nepal living in and around the Boston area.

name: Greater Charlotte Nepalese Association (GCNA)
description: The primary purpose of GCNA is to strengthen the community bonds and promote the welfare of Nepalese community in the Greater Charlotte Region and neighboring communities and/or states through various charitable and non-profit (Social, cultural and educational) activities.

name: Greater Saint Louis Nepalese Community (GSTLNC)
description: The primary purpose of this organization is to strengthen the community bonds within Nepali community in the Greater Saint Louis Area and facilitate the social togetherness through different charitable and non-profit activities.

name: Gujarati Association of Western Massachusetts - GAWM
Gujarati Cultural Association of Bay Area
Gujarati Samaj of Buffalo / Western New York
Gujarati Samaj of North East Florida
Hami Nepali Global
description: Hami Nepali Global was established as a non-political, non-ethnic and non-religious organization with the intention of tying all the Nepalese together in a single garland from around the world.

name: Heart Women & Girls
description: HEART is a national non-profit organization that works to ensure that Muslims have the language, resources and choice to nurture sexual health and confront sexual violence. We envision a world free from sexual violence where we live, work, and pray.

name: Himalaya Health Clinic (HHC)
description: Himalaya Health Clinic (HHC) is a free medical and dental clinic for the uninsured and underprivileged patients in the Nepali Community.

name: Himalayan Cultural Academy
description: Through the mantra that "music is medicine," HCA aims to demonstrate how integrating meditation and spiritual music into daily routines could alleviate mental and physical fatigue, fostering overall well-being.

name: India Culture Society NJ
description: Inspired by Indian moral leader, Mohandas K. Gandhi, the India Cultural Society (ICS) operates a Social Services Center named after the Mahatma and a Hindu Temple for religious worship.

name: India Currents
description: India Currents is an award-winning, nonprofit news organization bringing Indian Americans stories that explain and explore living in the United States. Established in 1987, we are the oldest newsroom dedicated to covering America’s Indian diaspora of 5 million, the fastest-growing Asian population in the United States.

name: Indian American Impact
description: OUR MISSION is to strengthen the rapidly growing South Asian legal community with a recognized and trusted forum for professional growth and development, and to promote equal rights and access to justice for the South Asian community.

name: Indian Cultural Association of East Alabama ( Auburn)
Indo-American Chamber of Commerce of Greater Houston
Indo-Caribbean Alliance (New York)
description: Indo-Carribean Alliance seeks to unify and advance Indo-Caribbean and South Asian interests by fostering joint and collaborative actions among different peoples, organizations and businesses in New York City. We empower our community by providing programs and services, creating cultural and education resources, and connecting our residents to government.  ICA serves as a welcome center for established and newly-arriving residents from Guyana, Trinidad and Tobago, and Suriname.

name: International Nepali Literary Society, INLS - Florida Chapter
Iowa Nepalese Association
description: Iowa Nepalese  Association is a non profit organization with the primary goal to share and promote Nepalese life and culture in Iowa.

name: Jahajee
description: Jahajee is a gender justice organization that activates Indo-Caribbeans in New York to put an end to intimate partner, family, and sexual violence. Our community of support guides women, girls, and gender expansive people to heal and create lasting change. Through arts activism, leadership development, and grassroots organizing, we amplify survivor voices and visions to transform culture.

name: Jakara Movement
description: The Jakara Movement is a grassroots community-building organization working to empower, educate, and organize working-class Punjabi Sikhs, and other marginalized communities; to advance their health, education, and arts/cultural, economic, social, and political power.

name: Kalakars
description: Kalakars is a collective of writers, filmmakers, and actors, striving to disrupt the status quo of South Asian representation in American film and television.

name: Kansas City Nepalese Society.
description: The mission of the Kansas City Nepalese Society is to preserve and promote Nepalese culture, and social educational activities within the community.

name: Karnataka Cultural Association of Southern California (SocalKCA)
Kasturi Kannada Cultural Society of NE Ohio
Kaur Life
description: Kaur Life is a registered, non-profit, online magazine, striving to be the premier Sikh Kaur’s publication. It is infused with gurmat and tailored for young Kaurs. Kaur Life hopes to be a space where Kaurs can express their ideas, share stories, and learn more about their Sikh culture to empower themselves.

name: Kerala Association Of Kansas City – KAKC
Kerala Cultural Society of Metropolitan Washington Inc.(KCSMW)
Khabar Magazine
description: Started in 1992, Khabar is one of the largest publications in the U.S. to serve Indian-Americans. They are a news organization that serves the southeastern US region and are based in Atlanta, Georgia.

name: Laal NYC
description: Laal provides resources Bengali womxn need to live healthy, engaged, joyful lives. We offer an array of programming, direct services, and resources to the vibrant Bangladeshi immigrant community.

name: Maharashtra Mandal of Jacksonville - MMJAX - Marathi Mandal
Maitri
description: Maitri is a free, conﬁdential, nonproﬁt organization based in the San Francisco Bay Area that primarily helps families and individuals from South Asia (Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Nepal, Pakistan, Sri Lanka, and the Maldives) facing domestic violence, emotional abuse, cultural alienation, or family conﬂict.

name: Malayalee Association of Central Florida – MACF
Malayalee Association Of Greater Houston – MAGH
Malayalee Association of North Florida, Inc. (MANOFA)
Malayalee Engineers’ Association of Houston, Texas
Mallige Kannada Association of North Texas
Manavi
description: Founded in 1985, Manavi is the first organization in the United States dedicated to ending all forms of violence against South Asian survivors. Using a survivor-centered approach, Manavi provides culturally specific and linguistically appropriate services to South Asian survivors of gender-based violence.

name: Mann Mukti
description: MannMukti is a storytelling platform that enables the South Asian diaspora to normalize and discuss mental health issues.

name: Marathi Kala Mandal of Greater Washington
MEANT (Malayalee Engineers' Association of North Texas)
MIT Heritage Arts of South Asia
Narika
description: Narika’s mission is to promote women’s independence, economic empowerment and well-being by helping domestic violence survivors with advocacy, support and education.

name: Nashville Nepalese Association
description: The mission of NNA is to promote Nepalese culture and heritage in Nashville and surrounding areas; to assist well-being of the people of the Nepalese origin; to assist people in times of distress.

name: National Federation of Indian American Associations (NFIA)
National Youth Organization of Pakistan (NYOP)
description: Our main operations are to organize Youth Education Programs, language programs, interfaith dialogues, and cultural integration programs.

name: Nebraska Nepalese Society
description: NNS preserves and promotes the Nepalese culture and harmonize interpersonal relationship and work on the holistic development of the residents of Nebraska and help them as and when needed while remaining a charitable and benevolent organization.

name: Nepal America Sociocultural Exchange Society, Inc, USA (Nepali Ghar)
description: Nepal America Sociocultural Exchange Society, Inc, USA (Nepali Ghar) serves our community by promoting congenial relationships and mutual respect among the Nepalese residing in Colorado as well as other local communities.

name: Nepal American Film Society (NAFS)
description: Nepal American Film Society (NAFS) is a non-profit organization based in Maryland whose mission is to bridge the world of arts and culture through cinema. The Film Society was founded by a group of media professionals, filmmakers and film enthusiasts in the United States. The Film society creates an international platform to promote film culture by organizing screenings, festivals, conferences, and exchange programs.

name: Nepal Center of North Carolina (NCNC)
description: NCNC is a center for Nepali communities to blend and foster relationships with other communities in North Carolina while maintaining Nepali culture and identity.

name: Nepal Seattle Society
description: NepalSeattleSociety (NSS) is a non-profitable, non-political and secular organization established in Seattle, Washington.

name: Nepalese American Law Enforcement Association
description: Our organization aims to strengthen the relationship between law enforcement and the Nepalese community through awareness initiatives that emphasize public safety, community outreach, and career opportunities in law enforcement, as well as encouraging the second generation of Nepalese Americans and community members to consider careers in government.

name: Nepalese American Nurses Association
description: The organization's primary goal is to unite all Nepalese Nurses and Nursing Students as a professional body under one umbrella at a national level. The  purpose of this organization is to help nurses who graduated from Nepal and America to get the credentials Evaluation Service, Licensing in different States and find jobs.NANA will also serve the Nepalese communities in United States through different health related awareness programs. NANA also prioritize to help not only Nurses in Nepal but also the people in Nepal through different educational projects and health related programs as well.

name: Nepalese Association of Colorado Springs (NACS)
description: NACS is dedicated to uniting and uplifting the Nepalese community in Colorado Springs, fostering cultural exchange, and promoting humanitarian values. We strive to create a vibrant, inclusive, and supportive environment for all, while preserving and celebrating our rich heritage.

name: Nepalese Association of Florida (NAF)
description: NAF's Mission is to promote unity and harmony among Nepalese communities in Florida by investigating and acknowledging diversity and common ground, as well as by providing a variety of programs, activities, and cultural events in order to preserve Nepalese customs, culture, and history in Florida and beyond.

name: Nepalese Association of Houston (NAH)
description: The main purpose of NAH is to serve the Nepalese community in the Greater Houston Area. NAH also strives at preserving and fostering the rich Nepalese culture, traditions, and historical heritage.

name: Nepalese Association of Indiana (NAI)
description: Nepalese Association of Indiana (NAI) is a non-profit cultural organization  with an objective to promote unity and collaboration among the people of Nepali origin, well-wishers, and friends.

name: Nepalese Association of Southeast America (NASeA)
Nepalese Association of Utah (NAUT)
description: Nepalese Association of Utah (NAUT) is a non political and nonprofit organization aiming in promoting Nepalese culture and unifying Nepalese away from home.We are an open organization where anybody can be a member. Nepalese Association of Utah is established to unite all the people sharing same Nepalese culture, language, and heritage.

name: Nepalese Society, Texas (NST)
description:
Nepalese Society, Texas exists to serve as an umbrella organization for all Nepalese organizations in Dallas/Fort Worth; to strive for the welfare of the community members in accordance with local and federal
regulations; and to provide support for relief and rebuild in case of natural catastrophes locally and globally.

name: Nepali Academics in America
description: NACA is an independent professional network whose goal is to build a community that helps promote the professional growth, interests, and well-being of its members through exchange of ideas, challenges, experiences, and solutions. It also helps foster research and educational collaboration within as well as outside of NACA for productive and impactful initiatives on education, research, and service including in Nepal.

name: Nepali American Center
description: The mission of Nepali American Center (NAC) is to build a community/cultural center in Chicagoland that provides a welcoming, inclusive, and supportive environment to celebrate and embrace cultural diversity for community members of all backgrounds. The center will be a platform for spiritual activities akin to Nepali traditions and provide support and opportunities for health and human services, education, and community building through a diverse range of programs, events, and activities. The center encourages mutual understanding and respect among its members and fosters cooperation with members from other communities, irrespective of their age, identities, race, ethnicity, and religious beliefs.

name: Nepali American Community Center (NACC)
description: The Nepali American Community Center (NACC) was established by DC Metro community members to preserve and promote Nepali identity, culture, languages, social and cultural values, norms and manners through the organization; as well as maintain and operate Nepali American Community Center to facilitate social, educational, cultural, spiritual and sports activities and programs aimed at promoting multiculturalism and social harmony in Northern Virginia.

name: Nepali American Friendship Association (NAFA)
description: Nepali American Friendship Association (NAFA)  is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit, community organization serving the Madison, Wisconsin metro area.

name: Nepali American Organization of Ohio (NAOO)
description:
The Nepali American Organization of Ohio (NAOO) is a non-profit social organization of Nepalis and friends of Nepal living in the State of Ohio. 
NAOO was started as Greater Columbus Nepali Community (GCNC) in 1999 to serve the Nepalis and friends of Nepal living in and around the Greater Columbus area.

name: Nepali Association of Northern California
description: The Nepali Association of Northern California (NANC) was formed as a non-political community with a purpose to promote and preserve Nepali cultural traditions, and language among the Nepali diaspora in Northern California.

name: Nepali Association of Oregon
description: NAO was established to promote and preserve Nepali cultural heritage and strengthen social bonds and collectively working for the welfare of the Nepali People.

name: Nepali Community Center Orlando (NCCO)
description: The mission of Nepali Community Center Orlando is to promote, preserve, and share Nepali culture.

name: Nepali Cultural Center (NCC) of Atlanta
description: The Nepali Cultural Center (NCC) is a non-profit organization based in Atlanta, Georgia (USA) and primarily serves the Nepali-American community in this metropolitan area. NCC’s goal includes the preservation and promotion of Nepalese identity and cultural heritage; establishment, operation and maintenance of a Hindu temple, a community hall, and facilities for conducting charitable, religious, spiritual, cultural, educational activities, and celebrating Nepalese festivals; maintenance and promotion of interreligious harmony, tolerance, goodwill, and understanding; and encouragement and support of humanitarian and charitable causes.

name: Nepali Pathshala of Virginia
description: Nepali Pathshala of Virginia is a non-profit organization established to raise awareness and provide education on the Nepali language, culture, arts, and tradition to the local communities in the Washington DC metro area. Our primary focus is younger generations of Nepali American community, however, we welcome anyone willing to learn about Nepali culture and heritage.

name: Nepali Public Relation Committee, America (NPRCA)
description: NPRCA advocates for democratic values, harmony, human rights and freedom of speech in our ever growing number of Nepali Diaspora in the USA. We promote Nepali Congress ideological values through our activities irrespective of political geography.

name: Nepali Youth Association
description: NYA aims to create a community of Nepali youth around the world and engage them in activism in social issues and other projects.

name: Nepalis and Friends Association, Arizona
description: Nepalis And Friends Association was established to promote social, cultural, educational, and many charitable activities of interest to the Nepalese community in Arizona,

name: New England Tamil Sangam (NETS)
service_area: New England: Connecticut, Maine, New Hampshire, Massachusetts, Rhode Island, Vermont

name: New Jersey Tamil Arts and Cultural Society (NJTACS)
New York City Bangladesh Civil Service Society
description: The mission of New York City Bangladesh Civil Service Society (NBCS) is to move forward the heritage, culture, and interests of the Bangladeshi Community in the greater arena of American Society. It shall provide an innovative institutional framework within which the City, State, and Federal government employees of Bangladeshi can interact with one another in good faith, with mutual respect and honor.

name: Newah Organization of America - Florida Chapter (NOA-FL)
Newah Organization of America (NOA)
description:
Newah Organization of American (NOA) is a non-profit organization to:
-provide a forum to advance the rich Newah heritage to secure the future for the coming generation
-advance the Newah community into the mainstream of American Life and foster strong ties with Nepal.
-enhance historical, socio-economical, cultural, language, literature, educational and techno-scientific bases of the community

name: Non Resident Nepali Association National Coordination Council of USA, North Carolina Chapter
Non-Resident Nepali Association National Coordination Council of USA
description: The “National Partnership for NRN NCC of USA” advances the integration of all Nepali organizations and NRN-NCC of USA to achieve a vibrant, just, and welcoming program for all the Diaspora members in the USA. Our vision is an authentic and welcoming approach for all the local and national Nepali organizations in which all NRNs achieve equal opportunity and are a powerful and organized constituency.

name: North American Bangladeshi Islamic Community (NABIC)
description: North American Bangladeshi Islamic Community (NABIC) is an organization that aims to improve the overall conditions of the people of Bangladesh and North America through charitable initiatives.

name: North American South Asian Law Students Association
Odisha Society of the Americas
description: OSA will continue to strive to be a focal point of all Odias around the world to debate issues and to find action plans in the nurture and promotion of Odia heritage and culture and matters meaningful to Odias.

name: Orisa Spiritual Assembly
description: Orisa Spiritual Assembly is a Hindu religious organization in Woodbridge, VA that serves the local community through spiritual practice and worship of Orisha traditions.

name: Pakistan American Association of Greater Pittsburgh
description: Our vision is to educate, emphasize and encourage Pakistani Americans to take part in the American Political system.

name: Pakistan Association of Greater Boston (PAGB)
description: PAGB aims to promote cohesion and unity among the Pakistani community through events, gatherings, and other social events.

name: Pakistan Association of Greater Seattle
description: Our mission is to bring Pakistani community together, enhancing Pakistan’s image and ideology in keeping with Quaid-e-Azam’s motto of Unity, Faith and Discipline and promote culture.

name: Pakistan Association of Memphis
description: PAM strives to improve the profile of Pakistan and Pakistani Americans in greater Memphis by organizing cultural, social, and humanitarian services.

name: Pakistan Information & Cultural Organization
description: To promote the cultural and heritage of Pakistan.

name: Pakistan-American Society of South Jersey
description: To establish a platform for a socially active community of Pakistani and American-born Pakistani residents in coordination with the local community.
primary_contact: President: M. Owais Lari; Telephone: 856-438-0040; Email address: passj@owais.net

name: Pakistani American Anjuman
description: Their mission is to provide cultural and social interaction for the families and individuals of the Pakistani community and to offer a variety of programs and services to its members.

name: Pakistani American Association of Connecticut
description: PAACT is a non-profit organization of Pakistanis living in Connecticut.

name: Pakistani American Association of Greater Washington
Pakistani American Association of San Diego (PAASAND)
description: PAASAND's mission is to promote Pakistani culture, heritage, and interaction with mainstream American Society. Also, dedicated to educating and engaging San Diego Pakistanis. Also, provide welfare services to the community of San Diego as well as in Pakistan.

name: Pakistani American Association of Tampa Bay (PAA)
description: PAA is is dedicated to promoting Pakistani culture, heritage, and traditions, as well as fostering a sense of community among Pakistani Americans in the Tampa Bay area.

name: Pakistani American Community Center
description: Our mission is to enrich the community through entertainment, education and outreach activities that preserve Pakistani culture and heritage.

name: Pakistani American Friendship Association
description: PAFA was founded with the mission of bringing the community together, staying connected, keeping our culture alive, and making a positive social impact.

name: Pakistani American Law Enforcement Society (PALS)
description: PALS's mission is to to develop and promote spirit of fraternity among all members of Pakistani descent serving in Law Enforcement Agencies in the United States.

name: Pakistani American Public Affairs Council
description: We support candidates who are committed to federal policies that promote good governance, education and security in Pakistan and civil liberties in the United States.

name: Pakistani American Skilled Women Organisation
description: We support and help needy women and families

name: Pakistani American Society of Greater Delaware
description: PAS aims to conduct charitable, cultural and educational activities, among others.
service_area: Philadelphia Metropolitan Area, Delaware, South NJ

name: Pakistani American Society of Minnesota
description: Our mission is to establish a collaboration and engagement platform for the Minnesotans of Pakistan origin.

name: Pakistani American Youth Organization
description: PAYO’s goal is to ensure youth in Midwood have access to opportunities and resources in order to achieve their goals as civically engaged Americans.

name: Pakistani American Youth Society
description: We strive to empower the youth and bridge gaps in the Pakistani-American community through communication, development and leadership

name: Pakistani Civic Association of Staten Island
description: Our mission is to help support and deepen our ties within the Pakistani community in Staten Island and beyond.

name: Pakistani Society of North Texas
description: PSNT is a non-political and non-profit social organization established to sponsor social, civic, educational, literary, scientific, charitable and religious activities for the benefit of the Pakistani-American community in Texas.

name: Pakistani-American Pathologists' Society
description: PAPS serves as a model platform to enhance the educational and professional development of Pakistani descent pathologists trained in USA.

name: Raksha
description: Raksha works towards healing, empowerment, and justice for South Asian survivors of violence that is culturally responsive. We work together with people from all countries, religions, gender identities, and orientations within the South Asian diaspora.

name: Ridgewood Nepalese Society
description: At RNS, we are dedicated to promoting Nepalese arts and culture, offering a range of programs to cater to various age groups and interests. Our community activities include language classes for children, cultural dance programs, sports initiatives, and festive celebrations that bring our community together. We are committed to supporting new Nepalese immigrants by providing language classes, settlement assistance, and a welcoming space for cultural integration.

name: Sadhana
description: We empower Hindu American communities to live out the values of their faith through service, community transformation, and targeted advocacy work.

name: SAKHI for South Asian Survivors
description: Sakhi for South Asian Survivors exists to represent the South Asian and Indo-Caribbean diaspora in a survivor-led movement for gender justice and to honor the collective and inherent power of all survivors of violence.

name: Sapna NYC
description: Sapna NYC transforms the lives of South Asian immigrant women by improving health, expanding economic opportunities, creating social networks, and building a collective voice for change. We recognize that women are the backbone of our families and that by empowering women, we are impacting whole families and uplifting entire communities. We strive to increase access for the women in our community – access to services, access to knowledge and information, access to systems, and access to pathways for social mobility.

name: Satrang
description: Satrang serves the South Asian LGBTQ community by promoting awareness, acceptance, and empowerment through social, educational, and advocacy-related events. We envision an inclusive and visible community in which South Asian LGBTQ-identified people feel whole and heard.

name: Seva International
description:
Hindu faith-based, humanitarian, nonprofit service organization registered under Internal Revenue Code 501 (c) (3). Founded in 2003, Sewa International is part of a larger movement that started in India in 1989 and is active in twenty countries. Sewa serves humanity irrespective of race, color, religion, gender or nationality.

We specialize in disaster relief and rehabilitation. Our development programs focus on family services; child, tribal and refugee welfare; women empowerment; health; and education.

In addition to our work in the US, we have undertaken development projects in Colombia, Guyana, India, Kenya, Pakistan and Sri Lanka.

name: Sikh American Legal Defense and Education Fund (SALDEF)
description: SALDEF is the nation’s oldest Sikh civil rights and advocacy organization focused on building leadership and capacity in the Sikh American community.

name: Sikh Coalition
description: Through the community, courtrooms, classrooms, and halls of Congress, Sikh Coalition works to protect the constitutional right to practice your faith without fear. We strive to do this with integrity, selflessness, and the belief that our shared work holds a greater purpose.

name: South Asian American Association
description: SAAAI was organized in New York City by a group of professional engineers more than fifteen years ago. Now this association is registered as a nonprofit corporation under IRS 501(c) (3). Each year we raise Scholarship Fund to help needy students pursue their education in the field of Architecture and Engineering.

name: South Asian American Digital Archive
description: SAADA creates a more inclusive society by giving voice to South Asian Americans through documenting, preserving, and sharing stories that represent their unique and diverse experiences.

name: South Asian American Justice Collaborative
description: SAAJCO is a dedicated legal services organization that seeks to advance justice and safeguard the civil, immigrant, and human rights of South Asians and persons of South Asian descent in the United States.

name:
South Asian American Policy &
  Research Institute (SAAPRI)
description: The South Asian American Policy & Research Institute (SAAPRI) is a non-profit, non-partisan organization established in 2001 with the mission to serve South Asian Americans in the Midwest by using research to formulate equitable and socially responsible policy recommendations.

name:
South Asian American Voices for Impact
  (SAAVI)
description: SAAVI is a non-profit, non-partisan community organization to serve specific issues of South Asian Americans through advocacy, education, and outreach efforts.

name: South Asian Americans for Change
description: South Asian Americans for Change (SAAFC) is 501c3 non profit organization committed to dismantling the stigma of mental health and promoting emotional, physical, and social wellbeing through arts, education and community-building events

name: South Asian Avant-Garde (SAAG) Anthology
description: SAAG is a leftist, internationalist literary magazine centering global South Asian solidarities.

name: South Asian Awareness Network
service_area: Michigan -- Student Org at University of Michigan

name: South Asian Bar Association (SABA)
description: SABA North America exists to strengthen the rapidly growing South Asian legal community with a recognized and trusted forum for professional growth and development, and to promote equal rights and access to justice for the South Asian community.

name: South Asian Bar Association (SABA) of Chicago
South Asian Council for Social Services
description: South Asian Council for Social Services’ [SACSS] mission is to empower and integrate underserved South Asians and other immigrants into the economic and civic life of New York.

name: South Asian Helpline and Referral Agency (SAHARA)

South Asian Journalists Association
  (SAJA)
description: SAJA represents 1,000+ journalists across the US and Canada and aims to improve the coverage of South Asia and the diaspora.

name: South Asian Leaders Unite to Empower (SALUTE)
South Asian Left Activist Movement (SALAM)
description: Our focus is on South Asia and its diaspora - to help build power in these communities. SALAM hopes to provide a medium for young South Asians to be political, especially young people becoming politicized now.

name:
South Asian Mental Health Initiative
  & Network
description: South Asian Mental Health Initiative and Network, SAMHIN, is a non-profit that addresses the mental health needs of the South Asian community in the U.S. Our goal is to decrease the stigma and shame associated with mental illness and offer resources for people who seek help.

name: South Asian Network
description: South Asian Network (SAN) is a community based organization advancing the health, emotional & mental well-being, and civil rights of South Asians in Southern California.

name: South Asian Public Health Association
description: SAPHA envisions better health and well-being of South Asians and the communities in which they live. The mission of SAPHA is to promote the health and well-being of South Asian communities in the United States.

name: South Asian Sexual & Mental Health Alliance (SASMHA)
description: SASMHA creates engaged communities for South Asian young people across the diaspora, focusing on adolescence to early adulthood. We seek to fight cultural stigmas, educate, and empower the South Asian American community by providing resources on the issues most important to us, from sex and sexuality to mental health.

name: South Asian SOAR
description: SOAR unites survivors, advocates, and allies into a national movement to end gender-based violence in the South Asian diaspora.

name: South Asian Youth Action (SAYA!)
description: Established in 1996, South Asian Youth Action (SAYA) is a youth development organization providing holistic, impactful, free, year-round programming to underserved New York City youth.

name:
South Asians for
  America
description: South Asians for America (SAFA) is a national, grassroots organization dedicated to the education, advocacy, and mobilization of the South Asian community to build South Asian political power nationwide.

name: South Carolina Association of Nepalese (SCAN)
description: South Carolina Association of Nepalese (SCAN) promote swelfare of all Nepalese living in South Carolina and Nepal through various social, cultural and educational activities.

name: Southwest Washington and Oregon Association of Malayalees – SWORAM
Subtle Curry Traits
description: Based off of Subtle Asian Traits, this is the largest South Asian Closed Community, currently at 1.1M people. It is a Facebook group dedicated to Internet memes, jokes, and discussion surrounding the South Asian experience in the West.

name: Tamil Cultural Association of North Carolina (TCANC)
TEC Nepalese Society
description: Our organization is dedicated to exclusively supporting charitable causes and nurturing the growth of the Nepalese immigrant community in the United States, with a primary focus on Maryland. We aim to enhance the cultural, educational, and overall well-being of Nepalese-Americans in our region while fostering strong bonds within our community.

name: Telugu Association of Florida, Tampa Bay
Telugu Association of Greater Charlotte Area (TAGCA)
Texas Nepalese Medical Organization (TNMA)
description: Texas Nepalese Medical Organization, TNMA, is a non-profit, nonpolitical medical organization of Nepalese medical professionals dedicated to provide free health clinic. TNMA was established to foster cooperation and cohesiveness in the medical community, to run free Health Camps efficiently, and to educate community members about health issues.

name: The Association of Performing Arts of India (APAI)
The Council Of Peoples Organization’s (COPO)
description: The Council of Peoples Organization’s (COPO) mission is to assist low income immigrant families, particularly South Asians and Muslims, to reach their full potential as residents of New York City. COPO empowers marginalized communities to advocate for their rights and understand their responsibilities as Americans. It helps to build community relations between Muslim and non-Muslim community groups. It continues to establish connections between the communities and various government agencies.

name: The Industry Show with Nitin Bajaj
description: The INDUStry Show is a widely broadcast talk show sharing global entrepreneurial journeys. Our mission is to educate, engage, empower and promote entrepreneurs.

name: The Juggernaut
description:
The Juggernaut is a media tech company and community that publishes well-reported stories about South Asia and South Asians. We call it “smart journalism for the South Asian diaspora,” though we welcome all readers who are curious about our stories.

We publish one new story a weekday and have now published well over 1,000 features, covering everything from politics to culture to business to tech. Our goal is to celebrate our heritage but also challenge what our history books and community have gotten wrong.

name: Triad Nepalese Community Center
description: Mission of the Triad Nepalese Community Center (TNCC) is to preserve the Nepalese cultural heritage by uniting the Nepalese community, delivering direct and supportive services for the community, creating opportunity for the community members and enabling them for competence and well-being in the Triad Area.

name: Triangle Area Telugu Association of NC
Triveni Mitra Mandal of Greater Cincinnati
Turning Point for Women & Families
description: Turning Point for Women and Families’ mission is to help Muslim women and girls affected by domestic violence to empower themselves and transform their own lives as well as those of their families. Turning Point provides a diversified range of culturally competent services focused on safety and self-sufficiency through direct services, advocacy and referrals.

name: United Sherpa Association (USA), Inc.
description: United Sherpa Association attempts to contribute the greater mission of establishing united and prosperous Sherpa community by developing common platforms for preservation and promotion of the unique socio-cultural and linguistic heritage.

name: Women Physicians of Association of Physicians of Pakistani Descent of North America
